examples.title: Truth Table Generator
examples.description: Generate truth table for a given number of input variables
examples.code:
ALGORITHM GenerateTruthTable
INPUT: n (number of variables)
OUTPUT: All 2^n combinations

BEGIN
  SET numRows = 2^n
  FOR i FROM 0 TO numRows-1 DO
    SET binaryString = ConvertToBinary(i, n)
    PRINT binaryString
  END FOR
END

FUNCTION ConvertToBinary(num, bits)
BEGIN
  SET result = ""
  FOR j FROM bits-1 DOWNTO 0 DO
    SET bit = (num >> j) AND 1
    SET result = result + bit
  END FOR
  RETURN result
END
examples.explanation: This algorithm generates all possible input combinations for n variables by counting from 0 to 2^n-1 and converting each number to binary representation. Each row represents one input combination.

examples.title: Boolean Function Evaluator
examples.description: Evaluate a Boolean function for all input combinations
examples.code:
def evaluate_boolean_function(expr_vars, expr):
    """
    Evaluate Boolean function for all input combinations
    expr_vars: list of variable names ['A', 'B', 'C']
    expr: lambda function implementing the Boolean expression
    """
    n = len(expr_vars)
    num_combinations = 2 ** n
    
    print(f"Truth Table for: {expr_vars}")
    print("-" * 30)
    
    for i in range(num_combinations):
        # Create input values as dictionary
        inputs = {}
        for j, var in enumerate(expr_vars):
            inputs[var] = (i >> (n - 1 - j)) & 1
        
        # Evaluate expression
        result = expr(**inputs)
        
        # Print row
        input_str = ' '.join(str(inputs[v]) for v in expr_vars)
        print(f"{input_str} | {int(result)}")

# Example: F = A AND B OR C
def F(A, B, C):
    return (A and B) or C

evaluate_boolean_function(['A', 'B', 'C'], F)
examples.explanation: This Python code generates a complete truth table by evaluating a Boolean function for all possible input combinations. It converts each row number to binary and passes values to the expression.
examples.spaceComplexity: O(2^n) for storing results if needed

examples.title: 2-to-1 Multiplexer in Verilog
examples.description: Behavioral and structural implementation of a 2-to-1 multiplexer in Verilog HDL
examples.explanation: Verilog code shows both behavioral (using if-else) and structural (using gate primitives) modeling of a 2-to-1 multiplexer. The testbench verifies all input combinations match the expected truth table.

examples.title: Boolean Expression Simplifier
examples.description: Program to simplify Boolean expressions using Quine-McCluskey method
examples.explanation: This C program implements basic logic gates as functions and evaluates a Boolean expression F = A·B + C for all 8 input combinations. It demonstrates how digital logic can be implemented in software.
examples.timeComplexity: O(2^n) where n=3, so O(8) = O(1)
